findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "studio_options" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: START HERE for any custom-print request: the menu of generation choices (print placements with plain-language descriptions, products, backgrounds, engines+costs…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "studio_generate" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Generate a CUSTOM t-shirt print from a text prompt (AI, takes 2-10 min, costs the user's Studio credits — a free starter balance is auto-granted on first use). …

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "studio_generate" is 1249 chars, far longer than this server's median (127). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Generate a CUSTOM t-shirt print from a text prompt (AI, takes 2-10 min, costs the user's Studio credits — a free starter balance is auto-granted on first use). …

description: START HERE for any custom-print request: the menu of generation choices (print placements with plain-language descriptions, products, backgrounds, engines+costs). Present these to the human — at minimum ask which PLACEMENT and which PRODUCT they want — before calling studio_generate.

description.name: studio_generate
description.description: Generate a CUSTOM t-shirt print from a text prompt (AI, takes 2-10 min, costs the user's Studio credits — a free starter balance is auto-granted on first use). IMPORTANT: first call studio_options and ask the human to pick placement + product (and optionally background); do NOT silently use defaults

description.name: studio_status
description.description: Check a custom print generation. Args: print_id (from studio_generate). When done returns preview_url — show it to the human.

description.name: studio_credits
description.description: The user's Studio credit balance. Check before studio_generate (1 credit per generation, either engine; welcome bonus 20; every bought tee adds 20).

description.name: add_custom_to_cart
description.description: Put a FINISHED custom print into the cart as a product. Args: print_id (must be done), product? (same set as studio_generate), size, qty?. Then the normal checkout flow applies (get_order_preview -> prepare_order).

description.name: designer_stats
description.description: The user's designer-program stats (if they are a designer-partner): units sold, revenue, accrued royalties, available for payout, royalty percent, their designer page URL.

description.name: designer_designs
description.description: The user's own published designs with live status (products on sale / hidden / pending). Args: limit? (default 50).

description.name: designer_submissions
description.description: The user's Studio publications in moderation: in review / in catalog / needs changes. Args: limit?.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "checkout_link" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Get the URL where the human opens their cart to review and pay. The agent never pays — checkout is always done by the person in the browser.
